title: Mapping object space dimensions: new insights from temporal dynamics
description: This dataset contains raw EEG recordings from participants performing a visual object recognition task designed to investigate how the brain represents object space dimensions over time. The study combines neuroimaging data with behavioral measurements to provide new insights into the temporal dynamics of object space coding in human perception.
methods_description: Data were collected during EEG sessions where participants viewed visual stimuli on screen. Raw EEG data were recorded in BDF format with concurrent behavioral trial data documenting stimulus presentation. Channel information and sampling rates are documented for each participant. The dataset is organized in BIDS format with raw EEG data files (.bdf) and associated metadata for each participant.
